data.title: Gold extends ‌losses to one-month low, now down 12% since Iran war started
data.description:
Gold futures fell for the third consecutive session Wednesday ahead of the U.S. Federal Reserve's decision to keep interest rates unchanged, matching traders' expectations; prices continued to slip after-hours following the decision.

Market attention remains focused on U.S.-Iran mediation efforts, and gold prices have tumbled more than 12% since the war began—after surging 65% in 2025—as rising energy prices caused by the cutoff of shipments via the Strait of Hormuz have revived inflation fears, leaving the Fed with less room to cut rates.

Because gold does not pay interest, higher-for-longer rates would make it less attractive as investors can earn more elsewhere.

"Only Trump and Iran can rescue markets, but the two sides are not getting any closer to a deal, and oil is reflecting that," Forex.com's Fawad Razaqzada said in a note. "Against this backdrop, the gold outlook is not looking so bright right now."

Geopolitical risks are not limited to the Middle East war, World Gold Council analyst Louise Street said in a note, with pressure also stemming from global trade tensions, uncertainty over U.S. monetary policy, and the war in Ukraine.

Meanwhile, the longer the Middle East conflict persists, the greater the potential risk to global growth and wider economic spillovers, which would typically strengthen demand for gold as a safe-haven asset, Street said.

The WGC said global gold demand rose 2% Y/Y in Q1, as a surge in purchases of gold bars and coins, as well ⁠as a ​3% growth in buying by central banks, outweighed a ​23% decline in jewelry demand.

Goldman Sachs maintains its bullish outlook for gold, saying it continues to forecast prices to reach $5,400/oz by year's end, as geopolitical uncertainty and concerns over fiscal sustainability could accelerate global diversification into gold, reinforcing its long-term upward trajectory.

The bank said it expects speculative positioning to normalize and anticipates ~50 bps of rate cuts from the Fed, reducing the opportunity cost of holding gold.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for May delivery fell 1% to $4,545.20/oz, and front-month Comex May silver (XAGUSD:CUR) dropped 2.2% to $71.569/oz, the lowest settlement value for both precious metals since March 30.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (DUST), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Leonardo DRS's unit wins $150M US Army FLIR sensor contract modification
data.description:
• DRS Network & Imaging Systems, a unit of Leonardo DRS (DRS), won a $150M contract modification to supply advanced 3GEN FLIR infrared sensor systems for major U.S. Army platforms, including the XM30 combat vehicle and Abrams M1E3 tank.
  • With this modification, the total contract value now rises to ~$213.1M from earlier levels.
  • The work will be done in Florida and runs until Jan. 2029, with about $20.2M in Army funds already allocated at award time.
  • The contract is managed by Army Contracting Command.

data.title: Inno Holdings to implement 1-for-20 reverse stock split in Nasdaq compliance move
data.description:
• Inno Holdings (Nasdaq: INHD) on Wednesday said its board approved a 1-for-20 reverse stock split of its common stock as part of efforts to maintain compliance with Nasdaq listing requirements.
  • The company said the split will take effect on May 4, 2026, and will reduce its outstanding shares to about 2.52 million from roughly 50.41 million, without changing the number of authorized shares.
  • Inno Holdings added its stock will continue trading on Nasdaq under the same ticker on a split-adjusted basis.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bessent bashes Powell for staying on the Fed board
data.description:
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent criticized Federal Reserve Chair Jerome ((Jay)) Powell for deciding to stay on the Fed board of governors after his term ends on May 15. Earlier on Wednesday,

Powell said he would stay on as governor for a period of time while there was still a possibility that a criminal investigation of him could still be reopened. "I will leave when it's appropriate to do so," he said.

"It's highly unusual for someone who says he's an institutionalist and cares about norms at the Fed," Bessent said during an interview on Fox Business on Wednesday. "This is a violation of all Federal Reserve norms."

He pointed out that the only other time a former Fed chair stayed on the board was at the request of the president. "I  can promise you that President Trump did not request for Jay Powell to stay," he said.

"I think it is an insult to Kevin Warsh, Mickie Bowman, and Chris Waller to think that these other Republican nominees do not care about the institution of the Fed and that he alone can maintain the integrity of the Fed," Bessent added.

Today Kevin Warsh cleared the Senate Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs. The Treasury secretary said he believes Warsh will be confirmed on time.  "I think it's going to be a new day at the Fed," he said. "We will have great monetary policy. I think we'll have great monetary policy. I think we will have great management in terms of the regulatory and of the facilities."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Viking Therapeutics net loss widens, cash position falls
data.description:
• Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) saw its year-over-year GAAP EPS loss in Q1 widen to ($1.37) from ($0.41).
  • The biopharma focused on metabolic and endocrine disorders was negatively impacted in the quarter by a near YoY tripling of total operating expenses to ~$164.1M from ~$55.5M. R&D costs accounted for ~$150.2M of the Q1 2026 figure.
  • At the end of the quarter (March 31), Viking had cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments of $603M compared to $706M on Dec. 31, 2025.
  • Viking provided updates on one of its key programs, VK2735, a dual GLP-1/GIP agonist for weight loss. Initiation of a phase 3 trial for the oral version is projected in Q4, and data from a maintenance dosing trial is expected in Q3.
